factors.name: Semiconductor earnings miss / AI demand concerns
factors.summary: Samsung earnings failed to meet 'high AI bar' expectations, triggering broad chip sector selloff (SMH -5%). VanEck Semiconductor ETF weakness rippling across Nasdaq; Micron shares down on AI tape skepticism per MarketWatch.

factors.name: US-Iran escalation & geopolitical premium
factors.summary: US launched 'powerful strikes' on Iran; Treasury revoked Iran oil sales waiver just 19 days after granting it. Strait of Hormuz traffic halted; ceasefire unraveling. Elevated geopolitical tail risk typically pressures growth-heavy tech multiples.

factors.name: Nasdaq-100 relative weakness vs S&P/Dow
factors.summary: Nasdaq down -1.16% (302.47 pts) on the day; S&P -0.45%; Dow tracking better. Nasdaq has never escaped negative territory during session, suggesting sustained institutional selling in mega-cap tech.

factors.name: AI capex bond selloff & funding stress
factors.summary: AI-related debt sold off sharply as Amazon announced $25B new bond issuance. Suggests capex cycle may be hitting diminishing returns or investor caution on AI ROI is rising; negative for chipmakers and cloud providers.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Fed's Williams sounding more dovish on inflation as oil prices retreat from Iran-war peak. No fresh Treasury data in news window, but geopolitical premium keeping rates bid; long-duration tech multiples under pressure despite dovish Fed rhetoric.

factors.name: Dollar strength on risk-off
factors.summary: USD Index holding near 101.00 area; AUD/USD dropped 0.39% on Hormuz tension. Stronger dollar headwind for US tech exporters (Nasdaq-100 has ~40% international revenue exposure).

factors.name: Fed policy expectations shifting slightly dovish
factors.summary: Fed's Williams comments and traders now hedging for less hawkish Fed (per Bloomberg); dot plot still shows majority expecting rate hikes by year-end, but momentum toward dovish surprise reduces odds of aggressive tightening.

factors.name: Trader positioning: Kalshi bets on cooler H2 2026
factors.summary: Speculators on Kalshi predict Nasdaq-100 will end 2026 above 30,000 but see more subdued rally in second half vs first half. Suggests peak sentiment may already have arrived.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is in a post-earnings selloff driven by Samsung's AI disappointment and broad semiconductor sector weakness; this is amplified by renewed US-Iran hostilities and risk-off positioning as Strait of Hormuz traffic halts. While Fed speakers have softened tone on inflation, the combination of chip demand concerns, elevated geopolitical tail risk, and AI capex funding stress is outweighing dovish Fed rhetoric. The index has spent the entire session in negative territory, suggesting institutional conviction toward lower prices. Kalshi traders see H2 2026 as cooler, hinting that the mega-cap tech rally may be entering a consolidation or correction phase.
catalysts_ahead: RBNZ OCR decision (Wednesday 8 July) – expected 25bps hike to 2.50%; any hold or dovish guidance would ease rate-hike cycle expectations globally
Major tech earnings this week (Tesla, Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple dates TBD) – critical to confirm capex cycle durability and AI revenue traction
Next CPI/PCE print – inflation surprise either way would re-price Fed hike odds and real yields
Strait of Hormuz status updates – de-escalation would ease oil premium and geopolitical VIX spike; further attacks would keep risk-off bid in place
Treasury yield moves – 10Y could spike on geopolitical premium; any sharp rise would compress growth multiple valuations
screening.risk_warning: This bias is invalidated if: (1) Samsung/chip weakness proves to be a single-quarter reset rather than sustained slowdown (watch Nvidia earnings for rebuttal); (2) US-Iran ceasefire is re-established quickly and Hormuz traffic resumes (oil premium collapses, geopolitical risk-off unwinds, dollar weakens, growth multiples re-expand); (3) Fed signals imminent rate cuts (real yields plunge, long-duration tech rallies 2-3%); (4) Major mega-cap tech earnings beat on AI capex ROI justification; (5) Next CPI comes in hot, causing market to reprice real yields higher and reduce fed-cut odds (initially BEARISH, but could reduce the immediate geopolitical risk premium). Watch 10Y Treasury yield and VIX closely—if VIX breaks above 25 and stays, 4h chart likely to show capitulation bottom.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (down -1.16% on session, index never escaped negative). Consider short entries only on intraday bounces to 29,400–29,500 resistance with tight stops above 29,600; safer entry is a pullback to 29,000–29,100 support zone if technical reversal signals appear. Avoid chasing lower into intraday lows as volatility and gap risk remain elevated into RBNZ and chip earnings.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector selloff
factors.summary: Samsung earnings disappointed despite AI hype, triggering broad chip selloff with VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) down 5% per CNBC. Nasdaq down -1.16% led by tech weakness, indicating AI trade taking profits.

factors.name: AI capex concerns
factors.summary: Micron stock fell on investor skittishness about 'AI tape' per MarketWatch analyst feedback. AI-related debt sold off sharply as Amazon announced $25B bond offering, suggesting funding strain for buildout.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: US launched 'powerful strikes' on Iran after Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks, with oil jumping and VIX likely elevated. Risk-off rotation out of long-duration tech into safe havens; Nasdaq particularly vulnerable to geopolitical spikes.

factors.name: Rate expectations
factors.summary: Fed's Williams sounded more upbeat on inflation but cautious overall. One-year inflation expectations hit highest since 2023 per NY Fed survey, keeping Fed hike pricing alive and pressuring growth multiples.

factors.name: China AI export restrictions
factors.summary: Beijing reportedly looking at curbing overseas access to China's top AI models per Stock Market News, adding regulatory uncertainty to AI trade and potential revenue headwinds for US tech operating in China.

factors.name: Mega-cap sentiment
factors.summary: Adobe called 'polarizing' amid AI questions per MarketWatch. Tesla sees 20% upside speculation on SpaceX merger but remains speculative. Micron and chip names under pressure signals broader tech rotation.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD surged amid Middle East tensions with AUD down 0.39%, gold retreating to $4,100 from Iran strikes. Stronger dollar pressures NDX multinational revenues and encourages rotation from growth to defensives.

factors.name: Oil price surge
factors.summary: WTI crude jumped on US strikes and revocation of Iran oil waiver, with Exxon reporting $3.7B profit surge. Higher energy costs feed stagflation fears, compressing tech multiples while boosting input costs.

factors.name: Options positioning
factors.summary: Traders on Kalshi think Nasdaq-100 will end 2026 above 30,000, suggesting cooler second half expected. Options traders betting broader market overestimates Fed hikes per Bloomberg, but near-term damage already done.

factors.name: ETF flows
factors.summary: Institutional investors exiting South Korean equities (KOSPI in bear market), tech-led strain per BNY. While Korea-specific, signals broader EM/tech de-risking that pressures Nasdaq components with Asia exposure.

confirmation.narrative: Nasdaq faces a confluence of headwinds: Samsung's disappointing AI-era earnings triggered a 5% semiconductor ETF selloff, exposing fragility in the AI trade that propelled YTD gains. Concurrently, escalating US-Iran conflict (fresh strikes, Hormuz vessel attacks, oil waiver revoked) sparked risk-off flows with oil surging and the dollar strengthening—both toxic for long-duration tech multiples. Rising inflation expectations (NY Fed survey highest since 2023) keep Fed hike pricing alive, while China's potential AI export curbs add regulatory uncertainty. The -1.16% Nasdaq close represents phase-2 selling; move is underway but not fully extended.
catalysts_ahead: RBNZ rate decision (likely hike to 2.50%) July 8—risk-off reinforcement if hawkish
US CPI print (date TBD but imminent)—critical for Fed trajectory and real yields
Iran conflict escalation trajectory—further Hormuz disruption could spike oil/VIX
Upcoming mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL dates unclear but Q2 season approaching)
Fed speakers this week—any hawkish tilt compounds pressure
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran ceasefire announced and oil collapses below $75, removing stagflation premium; (2) Fed speakers turn dovish citing growth slowdown, collapsing real yields; (3) NVDA or major hyperscaler issues bullish AI demand update reversing chip selloff; (4) US CPI comes in meaningfully softer than expected, triggering rate-cut repricing. Reclaim of 29,400 with conviction would suggest dip-buying overwhelms macro concerns.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-302 points today); wait for relief bounce to 29,300-29,400 resistance zone before considering shorts, or break below 28,850 support for trend continuation confirmation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a classic risk-off scenario: US-Iran escalation spiked oil to $72+ WTI, triggering stagflation concerns that compress long-duration tech multiples. The dollar bid (+0.10% on DXY) and bond selloff amplify the headwind. AI capex optimism has faded into skepticism (Micron, Adobe, Samsung rotation), and your prior SELL 7/10 bias remains intact. The 4h decline of 0.43% signals momentum is already turning south, but 24h gain of +0.38% suggests the move is early-stage (PHASE_2) rather than fully extended. FOMC minutes and Fed commentary are imminent catalysts that could introduce volatility.

Also watch for mega-cap earnings beats (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) — a single 'blowout guidance' could override macro headwinds and trigger short-covering rally.

If NAS100 breaks 29050 support cleanly on high volume post-FOMC, consider scaling into shorts. Conversely, hold shorts only if oil remains above $71 and dollar sustains DXY >101.00.

factors.summary: Fed Minutes expected to reveal internal debate on rate path under Warsh. Hawkish surprise would cement rate-hike expectations; dovish surprise unlikely given inflation re-emergence. High binary risk caps conviction; markets typically chop into releases.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is facing a fresh headwind from renewed US-Iran geopolitical tensions, with Trump declaring the ceasefire 'over' and military strikes triggering oil volatility (Brent +2.7% to $76.38, WTI to $73.10). This reignites inflation concerns at a critical juncture: the Fed's June minutes are due today, and market pricing is shifting toward higher-for-longer rates rather than cuts. Long-duration tech—the core of NDX—is acutely vulnerable to real yield compression (oil-driven inflation reducing real returns). Concurrent risk-off positioning (USD safe-haven rally, equity rotation to defensive names) and profit-taking in AI names (chipmaker exits noted) are reinforcing downside pressure. Prior three Haiku reads (all SELL 6/10, no confirmation) suggest weak conviction, but today's catalyst (FOMC minutes + oil dynamics) is creating fresh headwinds.

screening.risk_warning: If FOMC minutes signal dovish surprise (patience on rates despite inflation) or oil falls sharply (ceasefire restoration), NDX bulls could re-enter aggressively. Conversely, if oil stays elevated and Fed signals prolonged tightening, downside could accelerate below 19,600 support.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not yet extended (risk-off selling started ~3h ago); consider short on retest of intraday resistance (~20,000) ahead of FOMC minutes, or wait for pullback entry after Fed release at 18:00 GMT if hawkish narrative solidifies.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Ceasefire Collapse)
factors.summary: Trump declared US-Iran ceasefire 'over' on 2026-07-08; oil jumped 6-8% (WTI +$4.85 to $75.45). Strait of Hormuz blockade risks now pricing 44% probability traffic won't normalize until 2027. Equities sold off ~400 shares in S&P 500 as risk-off mood intensified.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates
factors.summary: 10-year note auction at 4.580% vs 4.586% WI represents upward repricing. Yields rising amid geopolitical uncertainty and hawkish Fed repricing. Long-duration tech (NDX proxy) highly sensitive to real yield moves; upside pressure on yields compresses growth multiples.

factors.name: US Dollar Safe-Haven Bid
factors.summary: DXY rallied as USD/JPY neared 162.50 (four-decade high). Safe-haven flows into USD typically weigh on equities with heavy international revenue (NDX mega-caps: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL). Dollar strength headwind for earnings translation.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations Re-pricing
factors.summary: Market now fully pricing another Fed hike by October per MUFG commentary. FOMC Minutes release at 18:00 GMT today (2026-07-08) will provide clues on Kevin Warsh's hawkish stance. Hawkish surprises would further compress tech valuations.

factors.name: Tech Mega-Cap Strength (Counterbalance)
factors.summary: Broadcom +3.5% on Apple's $30B chipmaking commitment (CNBC, 2026-07-08). Chipmakers bounced despite broad equity selloff. AI capex narrative still intact; hyperscaler spending resilient. Some offset to bearish macro.

factors.name: Energy/Commodity Inflation Risk
factors.summary: Oil rallying on Hormuz tensions; Russia banned diesel exports post-Ukraine refinery attacks. Stagflationary backdrop (higher energy costs, potential PCE re-acceleration) pressures both growth outlook and Fed policy. Brent crude +8%, typically inflationary for equities.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.41% in 4h and +0.41% in 24h — minimal move despite headline risks. Suggests limited downside conviction so far; market may still be digesting news or awaiting FOMC Minutes before committing direction.

factors.name: Bias History (SELL6/10 repeated 3x)
factors.summary: Prior three reads (10:15, 12:20, 15:21 on 2026-07-08) all SELL6/10 with no confirmation triggered. Suggests elevated risk but hesitation to commit. Bias is consistent; today's geopolitical escalation may finally trigger confirmation break lower.

factors.name: FOMC Minutes Imminent (18:00 GMT today)
factors.summary: Major catalyst hours away. Warsh's hawkish hold expected, but details on taper/hike trajectory unknown. Market likely to chop/consolidate ahead; risk of surprise repricing either direction. Caps conviction until after release.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (BTC Weakness)
factors.summary: Bitcoin slid toward $61k on ceasefire collapse and oil shock (CoinDesk, 2026-07-08). BTC-NDX linkage on risk-off days suggests further crypto pressure would signal broader risk-off, headwind for tech sentiment.

screening.narrative: NAS100 faces a critical geopolitical crosswind: US-Iran ceasefire collapse, oil spiking 6-8%, and safe-haven flows into USD all weigh on growth multiples amid rising Treasury yields (10Y at 4.58% bid). Mega-cap tech (Broadcom +3.5% on Apple capex deal) offers tactical offset, but the macro backdrop—hawkish Fed repricing, stagflationary energy risks, and yield compression on long-duration equities—tilts the risk-reward to the downside. FOMC Minutes dropping at 18:00 GMT today (high risk of hawkish surprise) and Strait of Hormuz blockade risks (44% chance traffic unsnarled only by 2027) keep risk-off sentiment alive. Prior three SELL6/10 bias readings remain unconfirmed; today may trigger break.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes release 18:00 GMT today (2026-07-08) — Warsh hawkish signals likely to compress long-duration valuations
Further US-Iran military escalation or ceasefire re-establishment (oil volatility driver)
Oil/energy inflation data flow into PCE/CPI expectations (stagflation concern)
Mega-cap earnings season updates on capex guidance (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings in coming weeks)
DXY continued bid / USD strength (headwind to international revenue mega-caps)
Equity options expiry / positioning unwind (gamma risk in NDX-heavy single names)
screening.risk_warning: FOMC Minutes surprise dovish OR oil moves reverse sharply (geopolitical de-escalation) could flip bias to BUY. Mega-cap tech strength and AI capex resilience could absorb yield shocks. China tech stimulus or AI deal announcement could reignite risk-on. Move is still light (±0.41%); lack of conviction suggests chop ahead of FOMC. Entry too early risks whipsaw into event.
screening.entry_guidance: Move in early Phase 2; confidence reduced to 5 due to FOMC Minutes hours away and light price action. Consider short on retest of 29300 resistance IF FOMC hawkish surprise occurs; otherwise, wait for pullback to 29050 support or post-FOMC consolidation before committing. High probability of chop into 18:00 GMT event release.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: Trump declared US-Iran ceasefire 'over' at NATO summit; CENTCOM launched dozens of strikes on Iranian infrastructure. Oil surged 7-8% (WTI $75.45), triggering broad risk-off across equities. Strait of Hormuz traffic not expected to normalize until 2027 per Kalshi predictions (43% probability by Dec 1).

factors.name: FOMC Minutes Hawkish Tilt
factors.summary: June 16-17 FOMC minutes (released 18:00 GMT) show 'a few' participants saw case for rate hike in June; most participants removed easing bias language. Upside risks to price stability remain elevated. Markets repricing higher-for-longer narrative, pressuring long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: Tech Sector Profit-Taking & AI Fatigue
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports >2/3 of tech stocks now 20% off recent highs; semiconductor names falling hard post-Q2 earnings. Micron investors demanding proof AI boom can sustain; Tempus AI initiated at Hold. Hyperscaler AI capex remains supported but sentiment deteriorating on valuation concerns.

factors.name: Treasury Yields (10Y Higher, Real Yields Sticky)
factors.summary: 10-year note auction at 4.580% vs 4.586% WI; yields coming in modestly on Iran commentary pullback, but core structural support from hawkish Fed. Real yields staying elevated, compressing growth multiples on mega-cap tech with long duration.

factors.name: USD Safe-Haven Bid
factors.summary: Dollar advancing for second straight session on Iran escalation fears; DXY grinding ~101.00. Stronger USD headwind for NQ components with heavy international revenue (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN). BTC declined to $62k on geopolitical/Fed caution, signaling broader risk-off.

factors.name: Negative Price Action Context (Small Moves)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.44% (4h), +0.38% (24h) — minimal gains despite major news catalyst. Move is choppy, not decisively lower, but not confirming strength either. This suggests exhaustion or consolidation into further downside.

factors.name: BIAS HISTORY Consistency
factors.summary: Prior reads on 2026-07-08 showed SELL5/10 and SELL6/10 with no confirmation triggered. Today's geopolitical shock and hawkish FOMC minutes confirm that bias; this is a refresh of an existing bearish lean, not a new reversal.

factors.name: Apple, Broadcom Mega-Cap Anchors (Positive Offset)
factors.summary: Apple announced $30B+ Broadcom chipmaking partnership; Blue Origin $130B valuation; BofA extended $520M loan to OpenAI. These mega-cap tech anchors provide some structural bid, but are insufficient to override macro headwinds.

factors.name: Nvidia Resilience (Bright Spot)
factors.summary: MarketWatch notes Nvidia as 'few bright spots' in rough Wednesday session; China allowing select AI firms to buy limited H200 chips. AI semiconductor demand remains intact, but sentiment is fragile on realizations that pricing power may not sustain.

factors.name: Fed Ambiguity on Rate Path
factors.summary: FOMC minutes show some hawkish language ('few' hike case, inflation risks elevated) but also no imminent move. Markets fully pricing another Fed hike by October per MUFG commentary. High uncertainty near-term keeps positioning choppy.

screening.narrative: NAS100 faced a sharp macro shock on Wednesday evening: Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire 'over' and threatened further strikes, sending oil surging 7-8% and triggering broad equity sell-offs. Simultaneously, the Fed released June FOMC minutes with hawkish tilt (inflation risks elevated, 'few' participants saw rate hike case), reinforcing higher-for-longer rate expectations. Tech sector is already reeling from post-Q2 profit-taking (>2/3 of names 20% off highs), and long-duration growth stocks are especially vulnerable to sticky real yields and USD strength. NAS100's muted response (+0.38-0.44%) suggests either dead-cat bounce or quiet consolidation before further downside. Bias History confirms prior SELL signals remain valid; this is a confirmation, not a reversal.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) — expected Friday; could modulate Fed rate hike expectations
CPI/Core CPI print — if hotter than expected, accelerates rate-hike repricing and headwind for NQ
Further Iran escalation commentary or Trump policy announcements — geopolitical volatility premium remains in play
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL earnings guidance updates — AI capex commentary critical to sector narrative
Oil price stabilization/reversal — elevated geopolitical premium could reverse if Iran tensions cool
Real yield (TIPS) moves — any further jump in real rates would intensify NQ selloff
screening.risk_warning: If Iran tensions de-escalate sharply (peace talks announcement, Trump pivot) and oil falls back to $70, risk-on would snap back and NAS100 could rally 1-2% intraday. If FOMC minutes are misread as dovish (which early market reaction suggests they're not), a correction higher to 29,500+ is possible. Finally, if mega-cap tech anchors (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) stage a surprise bounce, NQ could clear 29,400 resistance and negate the bearish bias. Watch VXN and NQ futures for confirmation of lower closes; if NAS100 closes +0.5% or better, bias downgrades to NEUTRAL.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (just started lower post-FOMC/Iran shock). Do not chase shorts here; wait for retest of 29,400 resistance or a pullback to 29,300 on any bounce before considering short entry. Alternatively, if NAS100 breaks 29,050 support cleanly on volume, that validates further downside toward 28,900.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: Trump declared ceasefire with Iran 'over'; US military conducted strikes on Iranian infrastructure; Trump threatened further action 'tonight.' Oil spiked >4% (WTI highest in 2 weeks), inversely pressuring risk assets and equity multiples.

factors.name: FOMC Minutes – Hawkish Tilt
factors.summary: June 16-17 minutes released 18:00 GMT show 'upside risks to price stability remained elevated,' most participants dropped easing language, and 'a few' saw case for rate hikes. Higher-for-longer narrative reinforced; USD supported.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield / Real Rates
factors.summary: 10Y note auction at 4.580% (vs 4.538% prior) signals sticky rates; FOMC minutes hawkish tone supports duration headwinds. Long-duration mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) extremely sensitive to real yield repricing.

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Support
factors.summary: USD/JPY near 162.50 (multi-decade highs); DXY grinding ~101.00 despite geopolitical risk; strong dollar typically headwind for US multinational earnings (heavy NQ exposure). Broader dollar rally pressures emerging markets and growth equity sentiment.

factors.name: Equity Market Close – Risk-Off
factors.summary: Dow Jones -1.09% on close 2026-07-08; Canadian S&P/TSX -0.95%. Broad risk-off sentiment despite oil/commodity spike, indicating equities repricing geopolitical AND monetary risk simultaneously.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Inclusion Weakness
factors.summary: SpaceX (new NQ component) closed below $148 debut IPO price in two-day slide post-inclusion. New large-cap addition to index underperforming is a minor negative structural signal; inflows may have reversed into weakness.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Capex Sentiment Mixed
factors.summary: Apple commits $30B to Broadcom (positive structural demand signal), but tech profit-taking and multipe compression from rising rates offset near-term upside. NVDA mentioned as 'one bright spot' but no earnings/guidance catalyst imminent.

factors.name: Inflation Surprise Risk – China CPI/PPI Tomorrow
factors.summary: China CPI and PPI data due 2026-07-09 (Asian session). Any miss could confirm global disinflation narrative, but Fed's hawkish tone suggests they're seeing persistent stickiness. Upside surprise would extend real-rate headwinds.

factors.name: Price Action – Minimal Intraday Momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 up only +0.12% in 4h and +0.60% in 24h despite geopolitical/monetary shocks. Lack of conviction rally into event risk (FOMC minutes, Iran escalation) suggests market hedging or distribution.

factors.name: Your Bias History – Sustained SELL Lean
factors.summary: 3 consecutive SELL reads (15:21, 17:28, 19:23 on 2026-07-08) with no confirmation triggers. This fresh read aligns with prior lean; suggests analyst conviction has been building into risk-off scenario.

screening.narrative: NAS100 faces a perfect storm: US-Iran escalation (oil +4%, geopolitical premium), Fed hawkish repricing (FOMC minutes higher-for-longer), and sticky real yields crushing long-duration tech valuations. Dow -1.09% and broad risk-off close suggest equities are repricing both geopolitical and monetary headwinds simultaneously. The index's flat intraday action (+0.12% in 4h) despite headline shocks indicates distribution or hedging; lack of conviction rally into FOMC minutes release is a red flag. Mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL) vulnerable to simultaneous duration and earnings-growth revaluation.
catalysts_ahead: China CPI/PPI data – 2026-07-09 Asian session (inflation trajectory signal)
US-Iran conflict escalation or de-escalation – real-time oil/geopolitical repricing

Fed speakers – any dovish surprise could flip bias
NFP, CPI prints later in week – inflation expectations reset
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran/US tension abruptly de-escalates or ceasefire restored (oil crashes, risk-on reversal); (2) Fed speaker signals pivot toward cuts (real yields crater, duration tech rallies hard); (3) China CPI surprises materially lower (disinflation narrative re-emerges, rate-cut bets rise); (4) Major positive earnings surprise or capex guidance from NVDA/MSFT/AAPL reignites AI rally. Geopolitical shocks are binary; positions should be sized accordingly.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (post-FOMC minutes, post-geopolitical spike); do NOT chase shorts into new lows yet. Wait for retest of 29,400–29,500 resistance before considering shorts; alternatively, scale into puts if NAS100 breaks below 29,000 on volume. Long bias invalidated unless oil reverses and FOMC tone softens within next 12h. High event risk ahead (China data, Iran developments); consider reducing position size into close.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran escalation)
factors.summary: Second round of US strikes on Iran overnight (reported ~20:18 GMT) targeting Strait of Hormuz, oil platforms, and drones. Trump stated ceasefire is 'over' and threatened further action including possible Kharg Island strike. Oil surged 3.5-7% intraday to $74.50+, reintroducing significant supply-chain tail risk for tech supply chains and hyperscaler opex.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Surprise (FOMC Minutes)
factors.summary: FOMC Minutes (released 18:00 GMT) showed 'a few' participants saw case for rate hikes; most participants preferred to drop easing-bias language. This hawkish tilt contradicts market's earlier dovish repricing and pressures long-duration growth multiples (NQ highly sensitive). Real yields likely to rise.

factors.name: Tech Valuation Under Pressure
factors.summary: BofA flagged Nvidia at 11-year low valuation (bullish call), but broader trend shows 2/3 of tech stocks down 20%+ from recent highs post-earnings. Sentiment turning defensive; profit-taking evident in insider sales (AppLovin director $1.6M, Palisade Bio CEO $303K, Contineum CEO $40K).

factors.name: Oil/Energy Inflation Spillover
factors.summary: Bloomberg noted gold holding decline as oil surge 'magnifies inflation risks.' If oil sticks above $74, CPI expectations reset higher, extending Fed hold or tightening cycle. Tech's leverage to long-duration multiples means tighter financial conditions = compression.

factors.name: Crypto Liquidation & Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin slumped to $62K (from $62.5K+ rebound attempt) amid oil spike, Japan contagion risks, and Fed tightening repricing. Ethereum flashed weekly death cross (worst signal in years per Cointelegraph). NQ-BTC correlation positive — crypto weakness often precedes equity pullback.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain Jitters
factors.summary: SK Hynix US IPO oversubscribed 7x (bullish for chip demand narrative), but Samsung/SK Hynix dominate 70% of Korea trading, raising concentration risk. South Korea economy exposed to Strait of Hormuz disruption; any supply shock ripples to TSMC, ASML, and US hyperscaler capex.

factors.name: Earnings Pullback & Guidance Caution
factors.summary: Levi Strauss beat and raised guidance (positive), but headline data is thin; most mega-cap earnings already reported. No major AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA guidance surprises in news window. BofA Nvidia call suggests stabilization, not momentum.

factors.name: China Growth Constraints
factors.summary: TD Securities reported China's fiscal stance turning austere as local governments prioritize debt cleanup. Asia inflation data (NZ manufacturing PMI surged to 59.7, China CPI/PPI due Thu) could trigger BOC/PBOC policy divergence, weakening growth outlook for US tech exporters.

factors.name: Dollar Strength Bias Crowded
factors.summary: MarketWatch reported investors haven't been this bullish on USD in a decade; crowded bet on dollar strength may face reversal if oil-driven inflation fears fade. USD/JPY above 162.07 (40-year high), but high positioning = risk of squeeze higher or reversal.

factors.name: Hyperscaler Capex Expansion Positive
factors.summary: Apple committed $30B+ to Broadcom US chipmaking; Meta building first Canadian data center; Paradigm raised $1.2B for AI/crypto. Long-term capex signals remain intact, but near-term opex pressured by energy inflation and geopolitical uncertainty.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has absorbed two major shocks overnight: escalation of US-Iran military strikes (oil +3.5%+, geopolitical tail-risk resurrected) and Fed hawkish FOMC Minutes (tightening bias, real-yield headwind for growth multiples). Despite only +0.60% gain in 24h and +0.12% in 4h, price action shows exhaustion after near-term chop; prior three SELL5/10 calls suggest momentum selling has not yet triggered a breakdown. Insider sales in tech (AppLovin, Palisade Bio, Contineum) signal caution from smart money. BofA's Nvidia valuation call provides tactical floor, but structural headwinds (inflation, Fed hold/hike, real-yield rise) argue against breakout into new highs. Oil stickiness above $74 is the key invalidator; if Strait of Hormuz fears ease by EOD or geopolitical rhetoric cools, pressure eases.

FOMC decision timing — if rate-hike odds creep above 20-25% by Friday, expect selloff in long-duration tech

Oil price hold/break above $75 or $80 — key barrier for sustained inflation narrative

Next mega-cap earnings season (late July) — guidance tone critical to multiple recovery
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran tensions cool rapidly (diplomatic breakthrough, ceasefire re-established), oil reverses sharply lower, inflation fears recede, and Fed pivots dovish again, NAS100 could gap higher 1-2% and invalidate SELL bias. Conversely, if Kharg Island strike occurs or Strait of Hormuz blockade tightens, CPI surprises hot, and Fed signals hikes inbound, SELL bias strengthens and support at 29000 breaks cleanly. Real-yields breakout above +0.80% on 10-year TIPS would also crush growth multiples; monitor closely.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 — FOMC Minutes just crossed, geopolitical shock fresh (last 4-6h). Avoid chasing shorts into overnight lows; wait for retest of 29300-29400 resistance or 4h/daily close below 29150 support to confirm breakdown before adding SELL exposure. If oil holds >$74 and VXN sustains >18, risk/reward improves.
